aliases: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
description: civil rights organization in the United States
description_wiki: The National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP) is an American civil rights organization formed in 1909 as an interracial endeavor to advance justice for African Americans by a group including W. E. B. Du Bois, Mary White Ovington, Moorfield Storey, Ida B. Wells, Lillian Wald, Emil G. Hirsch and Henry Moskowitz.

description: American teacher (1905–1951)
description_wiki:
Harry Tyson Moore (November 18, 1905 – December 25, 1951) was an African-American educator, a pioneer leader of the civil rights movement, founder of the first branch of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP) in Brevard County, Florida, and president of the state chapter of the NAACP.
Harry T. Moore and his wife, Harriette Moore, also an educator, were the victims of a bombing of their home in Mims, Florida on Christmas night 1951.

description: census designated place in Brevard County, Florida, United States
description_wiki: Mims is a census-designated place (CDP) holding the single zip code 32754 located within Brevard County, Florida, United States.

description: American law enforcement administrator (1895–1972)
description_wiki: John Edgar Hoover (January 1, 1895 – May 2, 1972) was an American law enforcement administrator who served as the fifth and final director of the Bureau of Investigation (BOI) and the first director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI).

description: organization that sought to promote social justice, civil rights, and electoral reform in the American South, particularly for African Americans
description_wiki: The Southern Conference Educational Fund (SCEF) (1942–1981) was an organization that sought to promote social justice, civil rights, and electoral reform in the American South, particularly for African Americans.

description: city in and county seat of Jefferson County, Alabama, United States
description_wiki: Birmingham ( BUR-ming-ham) is a city in the north central region of Alabama, United States.

description: Birmingham, Alabama public safety commissioner during the Civil Rights Movement (1897-1973)
description_wiki: Theophilus Eugene "Bull" Connor (July 11, 1897 – March 10, 1973) was an American politician who was Commissioner of Public Safety for the city of Birmingham, Alabama, for more than two decades.

description: North American labor union
county seat of Mobile County, Alabama, United States
description_wiki: Mobile ( moh-BEEL, French: [mɔbil] ) is a city and the county seat of Mobile County, Alabama, United States.

description: capital city of Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Atlanta is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Georgia.

description: independent city in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Norfolk (locally   NOR-fək) is an independent city in the U.S. commonwealth of Virginia.

description: largest city in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

aliases: University of Maryland at College Park
description: public research university in the city of College Park in Prince George's County, Maryland, USA
description_wiki: The University of Maryland, College Park (University of Maryland, UMD, or simply Maryland) is a public land-grant research university in College Park, Maryland, United States, near Washington, D.C. Founded in 1856, UMD is the flagship institution of the University System of Maryland and is the largest university in the state of Maryland.

surface_forms: 50,000 black people in Montgomery, Alabama walking instead of riding on a segregated bus
description: protest campaign against racial segregation on buses in Montgomery, Alabama
description_wiki: The Montgomery bus boycott was a political and social protest campaign against the policy of racial segregation on the public transit system of Montgomery, Alabama.

description: city in and county seat of Montgomery County, and capital of the State of Alabama, United States
description_wiki: Montgomery is the capital city of the U.S. state of Alabama and the seat of Montgomery County.

description: African-American civil rights organization
description_wiki: The Southern Christian Leadership Conference (SCLC) is an African-American civil rights organization based in Atlanta, Georgia.

surface_forms: Greensboro and a sister was in school somewhere else
description: city in North Carolina, United States of America
description_wiki: Greensboro ( ; locally ) is a city in Guilford County, North Carolina, United States, and its county seat.

surface_forms: I happened to have gone to school in Raleigh
description: city in and state capital of North Carolina, United States of America
description_wiki: Raleigh (  RAH-lee) is the capital city of the U.S. state of North Carolina.

description: county seat of Durham County, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Durham ( DURR-əm) is a city in the U.S. state of North Carolina.

description: American university
description_wiki: Shaw University is a private historically Black university in Raleigh, North Carolina.

description: United States civil rights organization
description_wiki: The Congress of Racial Equality (CORE) is an African-American civil rights organization in the United States that played a pivotal role for African Americans in the civil rights movement.

description: largest student-led civil rights organization during the American Civil Rights Movement
description_wiki: The Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee, and later the Student National Coordinating Committee (SNCC, pronounced  SNIK), was the principal channel of student commitment in the United States to the civil rights movement during the 1960s.
